label: Selling Photography of Lake Ontario Ducks

comments.text: If you ever get the chance to visit some Long-tailed Ducks I definitely recommend it. They are fabulous! (Found in winter on the northern coasts of North America and the Great Lakes) #birds 🌿
DO IT OREGON!!! BRING THE GIANT INFLATABLE DUCK TO LAKE MICHIGAN
A red-breasted merganser swallows a round goby, this morning in Lake Michigan near Montrose Pier
We learned that Oregon is playing Northwestern at Martin Stadium on Lake Michigan and we demand Oregon bring the giant inflatable duck.
OREGON FOOTBALL IT IS TIME THIS WEEKEND. BRING THE INFLATABLE DUCK TO LAKE MICHIGAN
With most of the water frozen over, any pocket of open water is packed with ducks. #birds #ducks #LakeErie

🪴 Hello!! I’m Ren or Birdy and I’m a digital artist who’s been around the furry fandom since 2009!
🪴 I have a particular fondness for hiking the Great Lakes, reading warrior cats, rollercoasters, astrology and meteorology!
🪴 Check my caard link for my other art profiles!
🪴 birdywren.carrd.co
A merganser diving into Lake Michigan. It was hunting for fish in the cold water. #birds #birdoftheday #seabirds #merganser #duck #seaduck #protectnature #wildlife #EastCoastKin #seabird

Ferris, one of three Great Lakes Piping Plover chicks that Chicagoans helped name in July, was attacked by a Red-tailed Hawk on Saturday.
Debating if I should paint a turkey or duck. And if duck what kind? Trying to stick to species in the Great Lakes region.
Here’s a muskrat and several long-tailed duck wintering in balmy Lake Ontario rather than their usual arctic home. 🌿🪶

I've had the good fortune & availability to get in a little #lifers birding between rainstorms this past weekend & came across two more. First is a Long-tailed Duck who wandered too close to the shores of Lake Erie. *All images heavily cropped. More in ALT 🪶🦉 #ohbirding #naturephotography #wildlife
A long-tailed duck (male) in Lake Michigan 🪶
Spotted a hooded merganser hanging out with a bunch of mallards! The ducks are officially on their way to Lake Michigan for the winter 🦆 #birds

This is a pretty confusing duck..for me.
A Long-tailed Duck for certain. Either a non-breeding female or perhaps an immature male.
A very northern breeding duck and common wintering duck on the north shore of Lake Ontario.
This should print to acrylic quite nicely.

A beautiful pair of Long-tailed ducks on Lake Ontario. These ducks are excellent divers who feed mainly on crustaceans and mollusks. #birds🪶 #bird #ducks #birdwatching #iloveny #nature #naturephotography

Some frosty Lake Michigan ducks to distract from this rancid heat. #birds

Great Lakes 🤩
These ducks are all common along here, but even downtown has great spots… great rafts of scaups or long-tailed ducks, buffleheads in among the goldeneyes, always a few mergansers and grebes in the mix. Elsewhere in my area, redheads, ruddies, the odd scoters, and more. I love winter!

My most favorite north american duck. 10s of thousands of them used to winter in the harbor at Sheboygan Wisconsin. Introduction of Zebra mussels in Lake Michigan screwed up their food resource and now Long-tailed Duck is a rare sight there in winter.
Ah yes; the duck scuppers of lake Michigan. Placed in strategic spots around the concrete storm walls.
Oh yeah, also shoutout to whoever put this object show art and Uncle Duck over Lake Michigan #wplace #bfdi #objectshow #teamroom125 #uncleduck
Is there a giant inflatable duck in Lake Michigan?
#LakeErieReport: open water (and a bonus red-breasted merganser!)

A promotional digital graphic for an online event titled Sacred Voices: Learning from Indigiqueer and Two-Spirit Leaders features a black-and-white photograph by Ryan Young (Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa). The image showcases two mirrored profiles of an individual
Last daylight petered out with little colour this evening over the lake. The clouds made for a dramatic contrast to the calmness of the lake. Long-tailed ducks called out in the deep water, unseen.

Redhead Duck.

A lot of Arctic birds are spending sometime in Lake Ontario on their way north. I think these are a common golden eye, a white winged scooter and a long tailed duck. We also have a lot of buffleheads.
